# Check current state after yellow right U click

for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['Y', 'S', 'O', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        if b[0][0] < 35:
            print(f"Red dot: ({b[0][0]}, {b[0][1]})")

But the light green bar is at cols 39-41, right next to the yellow bar. If the yellow bar can't grow further (it's at max width), then clicking the right U does nothing.
So I need another way to move the red dot rightward. Let me think...

Let me try clicking the orange RIGHT U-shape again. Earlier it triggered an animation but no board change. Maybe I need to click it multiple times, or maybe it only works when other conditions are met.
